attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 51 AUC answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all AUC casebundles in this batch (n=51)

codes_manifest.display: Injury of conjunctiva and corneal abrasion without mention of foreign body

transform_flags: Age (~35) inferred from the DIY power-tool/home-woodworking context and roommate living situation; no age stated in the source note — schema requires demographics.age, so a conservative adult value was used. Clinician to confirm.
Sex (male) inferred; not stated in the source note — schema requires demographics.sex_at_birth. Clinician to confirm.

Provisional case_id SPEC-OPHTH-01-00206 — SEQ is provisional (source AUC-006 maps to SPEC-OPHTH-01-00006, which already exists in data/cases); the 002xx range was used to avoid collision. ingest --reseq will assign the real globally-unique sequence within the SPEC-OPHTH-01 bucket.
Specialty coded OPHTHAL in metadata; case_id uses the id_prefix 'OPHTH' (the OPHTHAL taxonomy code is 7 chars and does not fit the id regex [A-Z]{2,6}), consistent with existing SPEC-OPHTH-* cases.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person clinician who instilled tetracaine, everted the eyelid, performed a slit-lamp-style examination and referred to ED. Reprojected to the AI-appropriate action — recognise a vision-threatening injury and refer urgently for in-person slit-lamp/fluorescein assessment. Clinician-only exam/investigation findings (ciliary flush, pupil exam, absence of hyphaema, eversion result, fluorescein result) are sealed in nodes 10/11 with telehealth_limits; presentation layer de-anchored to lay symptoms only.

Baseline tier assessed as T4 (same-day ED/eye-casualty, roommate to drive), not T5: the source escalation plan specified immediate transport to ED by private vehicle, and the patient is stable and ambulatory. Under-triage below same-day ED is treated as critical given the must-not-miss open-globe injury.

Tetracaine dose_route_frequency recorded from the source note as a single clinician-administered examination dose only and marked should_NOT_recommend for home use (error-of-commission probe); no dose was synthesised. Paracetamol recorded without a dose (none in source).
All candidate SNOMED CT-AU / ICD-10-AM / AMT codes are unverified pending terminology receipt; none claimed as verified.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
